name: paper-hyperconn-limitations
description: Use web_search to search for 'hyper-connections limitations training instability identity mapping scalability'. Results overlap with earlier searches. What are the main limitations of hyper-connections? List 3 issues, one sentence each. Keep response under 80 words.

ground_truth.reference_answer:
1. Training instability: Diversifying residual streams compromises the identity mapping property, causing gradient explosions and loss spikes (observed around 12k training steps), limiting scalability to very large models.
2. Memory overhead: The n-stream residual design increases memory I/O quadratically with expansion rate n, creating substantial system-level overhead.
3. Loss of identity mapping: Learned connection matrices can drift far from identity, disrupting stable signal propagation across deep layers and degrading performance at large scales.
key_facts: Training instability from loss of identity mapping property causes gradient explosions and loss spikes
Memory access overhead scales quadratically with expansion rate n
Scalability is limited for very large models without additional constraints like mHC
Loss surges observed around 12k training steps correlate with gradient norm instability
These limitations motivated DeepSeek's follow-up work on Manifold-Constrained Hyper-Connections (mHC)
